pattern: Precomputing a palindrome table for all substrings turns the three-way split into a check over two cut positions.
hints: You only need to know, for every pair of indices, whether the enclosed substring is a palindrome.
Once palindrome lookups are O(1), try every pair of cut points that yields three non-empty pieces.
Fill a 2D table where pal[i][j] is true if s[i..j] reads the same both ways, then search cuts i<j so s[0..i-1], s[i..j-1], s[j..] are all palindromes.

edge_inputs.description: cannot be split into three palindromes
minimum length three of same letter
minimum length all distinct still works as singletons
four distinct letters cannot make three palindromes

stress.description: random short lowercase strings over a tiny alphabet to hit both outcomes
